relations.subject: Pink-bellied imperial pigeon
relations.evidence: The pink-bellied imperial pigeon (Ducula poliocephala), also known as the zone-tailed pigeon, is found in the Philippines

relations.subject: Pink-bellied imperial pigeon
relations.evidence: Its natural habitat is tropical moist lowland forest and montane forest up to 1,500 m
relations.object_summary: 以成熟阔叶林和混交林为主的山地森林系统。

relations.subject: Pink-bellied imperial pigeon
relations.evidence: It is threatened by habitat loss. It is illegal to hunt, capture or possess these birds under Philippine Law RA 9147.{{Cite web |last=11th Congress |title=Republic Act No
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

relations.subject: Pink-bellied imperial pigeon
relations.evidence: According to the IUCN Red List and BirdLife International's Red Book, the pink-bellied imperial pigeon is classified as Near-threatened. However, The National List of Threatened Terrestrial Fauna of the Philippines has classified it as Critically Endangered. thumb This is due to the loss of habitat, trapping for the pet trade and hunting for food
relations.object_summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

relations.subject: Pink-bellied imperial pigeon
relations.evidence: Although it occurs quite widely within the Philippines, this species is scarce and probably has a moderately small population, which is suspected to be in moderately rapid decline owing to hunting and the removal of its favoured lowland forest habitat. This bird is extremely rare in Luzon but frequently more commonly observed in Negros Island, Mindoro, Samar and Mindanao
relations.object_summary: 种群规模过小导致遗传多样性下降和脆弱性上升。
